desc: KPIs financeiros, análise de investimentos e linguagem de P&L.

cards.pt: LAJIDA (Lucro antes de juros, impostos, depreciação e amortização)
cards.def: Earnings Before Interest, Taxes, Depreciation and Amortization — a proxy for operational cash profitability.
cards.ex: The acquisition target had a robust EBITDA margin of 28%, making it attractive despite elevated debt levels.
cards.tip: Pronuncia: "ee-BIT-dah". EBITDA margin = EBITDA ÷ Revenue. McKinsey usa como proxy de geração de caixa operacional.

cards.def: Net Present Value: the difference between the present value of cash inflows and outflows over a project's life.
cards.ex: The NPV analysis confirmed the $40M investment would yield a positive return of $18M over five years.
cards.tip: NPV > 0 = cria valor. Fórmula: Σ(CFₜ/(1+r)ᵗ) − C₀. Taxa de desconto usada: WACC da empresa.

cards.def: The rate at which an organization consumes available cash, typically expressed as a monthly figure.
cards.ex: With a $500K monthly burn rate and $2M in reserves, the startup had a four-month runway.
cards.tip: Runway = caixa disponível ÷ burn rate. Crítico em startups e projetos com capital limitado.

cards.def: Return on Investment: a ratio evaluating the efficiency of an investment relative to its cost.
cards.ex: The digital transformation delivered an ROI of 340% over three years, far exceeding the 150% target.
cards.tip: Fórmula: (Ganho − Custo) ÷ Custo. Simples mas poderoso em executive presentations e business cases.

cards.def: Capital Expenditures: funds used to acquire, upgrade, or maintain long-term physical or intangible assets.
cards.ex: The factory expansion required $12M in CAPEX, subject to board approval at the investment committee.
cards.tip: CAPEX → ativo no balanço, depreciado ao longo do tempo. OPEX → despesa no P&L corrente.

cards.def: Operating Expenditures: the ongoing costs required to run a business or project day-to-day.
cards.ex: Migrating to cloud shifted substantial CAPEX to predictable OPEX, improving cash flow visibility.
cards.tip: Cloud migration é o exemplo clássico de CAPEX→OPEX shift. OPEX aparece no P&L como despesa do período corrente.

cards.def: The net movement of cash and cash equivalents into and out of a business over a specific period.
cards.ex: Despite strong revenues, poor cash flow management led the profitable firm to miss payroll cycles.
cards.tip: Revenue ≠ Cash Flow. Empresas lucrativas podem ir à falência por caixa insuficiente.

cards.def: A systematic comparison of all costs and expected benefits to determine whether an option is worthwhile.
cards.ex: The cost-benefit analysis justified the $2M upgrade by projecting $7M in annual savings over four years.
cards.tip: Ferramenta decisiva em business cases. Inclui tangible e intangible benefits. Sensitivity analysis complementa.

cards.def: The difference between budgeted and actual amounts, indicating over- or underspending.
cards.ex: A 12% unfavorable budget variance in Q3 triggered an urgent review of procurement practices.
cards.tip: Favorable = abaixo do orçado (bom). Unfavorable/Adverse = acima do orçado. Distinção importante em reporting.

cards.def: The use of debt or borrowed capital to amplify potential investment returns, also magnifying losses.
cards.ex: The PE firm leveraged the acquisition at 5x EBITDA, expecting improvements to service the debt load.
cards.tip: Financial leverage = dívida/equity. Operating leverage = custos fixos vs variáveis. Ambos amplificam resultados.

cards.def: The gradual reduction of an intangible asset's value or debt balance over time through scheduled charges.
cards.ex: The $6M software license was amortized over five years, smoothing the cost impact across operating periods.
cards.tip: Amortization = intangíveis (patentes, software, goodwill). Depreciation = ativos tangíveis. Confusão comum.

cards.def: A dynamic projection of future financial performance based on current data, trends, and updated assumptions.
cards.ex: The revised Q3 forecast showed a 15% revenue shortfall, prompting targeted cost mitigation measures.
cards.tip: Budget (plano fixo, anual) ≠ Forecast (projeção dinâmica, revisada). Rolling forecast = best practice em FP&A.
